scenario: Axum Handler trait propagation and request context layer assessment

map_quality.rationale: Lists 7 Handler impls with exact file:line (mod.rs:208, 227, 271, 317; method_routing.rs:1355; axum-extra or.rs:56, IntoHandler at mod.rs:167) plus T/S markers and where the user closure is invoked. Also surfaces HandlerService and HandlerWithoutStateExt — an agent could pick the right impl to extend without grepping.

specificity.rationale: Cites the macro expansion explicitly: `all_the_tuples!` at macros.rs:49 with the 1..=16 expansion and mod.rs:262 invocation, plus concrete bounds like `FnOnce(T1..Tn, Last) -> Fut` and the `M` marker for the `FromRequest<S, M>` variant.

justification.rationale: Explains WHY each impl exists — IntoResponseHandler ignores req, Layered delegates via Tower oneshot, MethodRouter dispatches by method — rather than just naming them. The T-marker explanation as a coherence workaround is concrete.

uncertainty.rationale: Flags axum-extra extensions (HandlerCallWithExtractors, Or, IntoHandler) and notes the tuple macro expansion. Doesn't explicitly caveat that external/user-defined Handler impls won't appear in this list, but coverage is otherwise thorough.

map_quality.rationale: Provides FromRequestParts at axum-core/src/extract/mod.rs:53 and FromRequest at mod.rs:79, 18+ built-in extractors with file:line (State, Extension, Path, Query, Json, Form, Multipart, MatchedPath, etc.), and traces the rejection path through Handler::call at mod.rs:244/252. An agent can wire a new extractor with only small lookups.

specificity.rationale: Exact names with file:line (e.g. `State` at axum/src/extract/state.rs:303, `FromRef` at axum-core/src/extract/from_ref.rs:14, blanket impl at mod.rs:91), and the FromRequestParts vs FromRequest distinction is concretized with the M=ViaParts/ViaRequest marker.

justification.rationale: Explicitly explains the split (`Cannot consume body — composable` vs `Consumes body — exactly one per handler, must be the last extractor`) and how State uses FromRef including the identity blanket impl and substate via `#[derive(FromRef)]`. Could expand slightly on why the M marker exists.

uncertainty.rationale: Flags subtleties like the `Result<T, T::Rejection>` escape hatch and central rejection module locations, but does not explicitly warn about user-defined extractors or pitfalls when wiring custom rejection conversions (e.g. axum-extra extensions). Calibration to 180s effort is reasonable.

map_quality.rationale: All 7 layers are walked with file:line at each — serve/mod.rs:103, handle_connection at :559, MakeService impls at routing/mod.rs:579 and method_routing.rs:1371, Router::call_with_state at mod.rs:452, PathRouter at path_router.rs:325, MethodRouter at method_routing.rs:1167, HandlerService at service.rs:146. An agent could place middleware at the right layer without re-grepping.

specificity.rationale: Exact method names throughout: call_with_state (not 'the call method'), set_matched_path_for_request at matched_path.rs:101, TowerToHyperService cited at the bridge, oneshot_inner_owned named for Route dispatch, builder.serve_connection_with_upgrades cited at serve/mod.rs:613.

justification.rationale: Explains role of each layer (why PathRouter inserts MatchedPath before dispatch, why MethodRouter dispatches via the call! macro, what HandlerService bakes in via into_route, IntoResponse at the boundary). Could be slightly more explicit on the Tower-to-hyper bridge's reason for existence.

uncertainty.rationale: Notes IntoMakeServiceWithConnectInfo as a variant and references the axum-core split implicitly via file paths, but doesn't explicitly flag axum-core vs axum boundaries or alternative serve paths (axum::serve vs hyper directly) that the agent should verify against.

steps.step: Assess adding a request context layer

map_quality.rationale: Concrete edit map with specific files (new request_id.rs extractor + middleware), exact insertion points (path_router.rs:347 next to set_matched_path_for_request, mod.rs:307 for catch_all_fallback), and re-export sites (extract/mod.rs, lib.rs:433). Agent could open a PR directly from this list.

specificity.rationale: Tradeoffs grounded in actual axum surfaces with file:line — Router::layer at mod.rs:296 vs route_layer at mod.rs:313 with the catch_all_fallback distinction at mod.rs:307, plus MatchedPath precedent cited at matched_path.rs:67 and path_router.rs:347. Not abstract middleware talk.

justification.rationale: Explains WHY dedicated extractor beats Extension<T> (TypeId collisions, ergonomics) and WHY Handler trait modification is breaking (six impl sites + n-arg blanket at mod.rs:227). The 'least_breaking_choice' rationale ties directly to backward compat.

uncertainty.rationale: Flags nested router interaction via MatchedNestedPath at matched_path.rs:99 and the fallback-coverage tradeoff between layer/route_layer. Could more explicitly call out tower middleware ordering concerns, but covers the major verification points.
